events.title: Presanctified Liturgy
events.subtitle: Divine Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts
events.description: The Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts is a vespers service combined with the reception of Holy Communion, served on Wednesday evenings during Great Lent. Confession available from 6:00 PM. All faithful are welcome.

events.title: Lazarus Saturday Vespers
events.subtitle: The raising of Lazarus of Bethany
events.description: Great Vespers for Lazarus Saturday, commemorating the miracle of the raising of Lazarus — the prefigurement of the Resurrection of Christ. A solemn service marking the transition into Holy Week.

events.title: Spring Food Drive
events.subtitle: Serving our neighbors in Jersey City
events.description: Bring non-perishable food items to donate to the Jersey City food pantry. We collect after Sunday Liturgy and deliver the same afternoon. Volunteers welcome for sorting and transport. All are invited to participate in this act of mercy.

events.title: Men's Breakfast & Discussion
events.subtitle: Monthly gathering for the men of the parish
events.description: Monthly men's gathering over breakfast. This month's discussion: "Fatherhood in the Orthodox Tradition." Open to all men of the parish and their guests. No registration required.

events.title: Byzantine Chant Evening
events.subtitle: An evening of sacred music from the Orthodox tradition
events.description: An evening of Byzantine and Slavic sacred chant performed by the cathedral choir and invited singers from the Diocese. Light refreshments to follow in the parish hall. Free admission — freewill offering gratefully accepted.

events.title: Thomas Sunday Divine Liturgy
events.subtitle: First Sunday after Pascha · Renewal Sunday
events.description: Divine Liturgy for Thomas Sunday, the first Sunday after Pascha, also called Renewal Sunday or Antipascha. The Gospel reading recounts the appearance of the risen Christ to the Apostle Thomas. Confession available from 9:15 AM.

events.title: Great Vespers
events.subtitle: Eve of Thomas Sunday
events.description: Great Vespers on the eve of Thomas Sunday. The Paschal greeting continues to be exchanged throughout Bright Week and into the Paschal season.

events.title: Adult Catechesis
events.subtitle: Introduction to Orthodox theology and worship
events.description: Weekly catechesis class open to inquirers, catechumens, and any parishioner who wishes to deepen their understanding of the Orthodox faith. This session covers the Divine Liturgy — its structure, theology, and history. No prior knowledge required.

events.title: Parish Coffee Hour
events.subtitle: Hosted by the Parish Council
events.description: Join us for fellowship after the Divine Liturgy. Light refreshments provided by the Parish Council. All are welcome — visitors and inquirers especially encouraged to come and meet the community.

events.title: Wednesday Bible Study
events.subtitle: The Gospel of John — Pascha through Pentecost
events.description: Weekly Bible study following the lectionary readings of the Paschal season. This spring we are reading through the Gospel of John, focusing on the farewell discourse and resurrection appearances. Open to all. Bring your Bible.

events.title: Diocesan Pilgrimage
events.subtitle: Holy Trinity Monastery, Jordanville NY
events.description: Annual pilgrimage to Holy Trinity Monastery in Jordanville, NY for the feast of St. Job of Pochaev. Bus departs at 6:00 AM and returns by 8:00 PM. Cost: $35 per person. Register with the parish office by May 2.

events.title: Spring Parish Luncheon
events.subtitle: Annual fundraising luncheon
events.description: The annual spring luncheon hosted by the Ladies Auxiliary. Tickets are $40 per person and support the parish general fund. Seating is limited. Silent auction to follow.

events.title: Great Vespers
events.subtitle: Eve of the Myrrhbearing Women
events.description: Great Vespers on the eve of the Sunday of the Myrrhbearing Women, the faithful women who came to anoint the body of Christ and found the tomb empty.

events.title: Sunday of the Myrrhbearing Women
events.subtitle: Third Sunday of Pascha
events.description: Divine Liturgy for the Sunday of the Myrrhbearing Women, honoring the faithful women disciples who came to the tomb on Pascha morning. Coffee hour follows.

events.title: Wednesday Bible Study
events.subtitle: The Gospel of John, Chapter 6
events.description: Continuing our study of the Gospel of John. This week: the Bread of Life discourse. Open to all, no prior sessions required. Bring your Bible.

events.title: Great Vespers
events.subtitle: Eve of the Paralytic
events.description: Great Vespers on the eve of the Sunday of the Paralytic, recounting Christ's healing at the Pool of Bethesda.

events.title: Sunday of the Paralytic
events.subtitle: Fourth Sunday of Pascha
events.description: Divine Liturgy for the Sunday of the Paralytic. The Gospel reading tells of Christ healing the man who had been ill for thirty-eight years. Coffee hour follows.

events.title: Youth Group Gathering
events.subtitle: For ages 12 and up
events.description: Monthly gathering for the young people of the parish. Lunch, games, and a short discussion on the lives of young saints. Open to ages 12 and up. Parents welcome to stay.

events.title: Choir Rehearsal
events.subtitle: Preparing for the Ascension
events.description: Weekly choir rehearsal. This month we are preparing the hymns for the Feast of the Ascension. New singers always welcome, no audition required.

events.title: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
events.subtitle: Fifth Sunday of Pascha
events.description: Divine Liturgy for the Sunday of the Samaritan Woman. The Gospel recounts Christ's conversation at Jacob's Well and the gift of living water. Coffee hour follows.

events.title: Mother's Day Brunch
events.subtitle: Honoring the mothers of the parish
events.description: A special brunch honoring all the mothers of the parish. Hosted by the Parish Council. Flowers for every mother present. All are welcome to celebrate together.

events.title: Adult Catechesis
events.subtitle: The Sacraments of the Church
events.description: Continuing our catechesis series. This session: an overview of the Holy Mysteries (Sacraments) of the Orthodox Church, with special attention to Baptism and Chrismation. Open to all.

events.title: Sunday of the Blind Man
events.subtitle: Sixth Sunday of Pascha
events.description: Divine Liturgy for the Sunday of the Blind Man. The Gospel tells of Christ restoring sight to the man born blind. Coffee hour follows.

events.title: Vigil for the Ascension
events.subtitle: Great Vespers and Matins · Eve of the Feast
events.description: Great Vespers and Matins on the eve of the Feast of the Ascension, one of the Twelve Great Feasts of the Church. This is also the Leavetaking of Pascha: the final farewell to the Paschal season before the Lord's ascent to the Father. All are welcome.

events.title: Feast of the Ascension
events.subtitle: One of the Twelve Great Feasts · Thursday
events.description: Divine Liturgy for the Feast of the Ascension of our Lord, God, and Saviour Jesus Christ. Forty days after Pascha, Christ gathers his disciples on the Mount of Olives, blesses them, and is taken up into heaven. The same body that was crucified and raised now ascends to the right hand of the Father. No fast today. All are welcome.

events.title: Vigil for the Transfiguration
events.subtitle: Great Vespers and Matins · Eve of the Feast
events.description: Great Vespers and Matins on the eve of the Feast of the Transfiguration of our Lord, one of the Twelve Great Feasts of the Church. We are in the Dormition Fast. All are welcome.

events.title: Feast of the Transfiguration
events.subtitle: One of the Twelve Great Feasts · Thursday
events.description: Divine Liturgy for the Feast of the Transfiguration of our Lord. On Mount Tabor, Christ was transfigured before Peter, James, and John: his face shone like the sun, Moses and Elijah appeared beside him, and a voice from the cloud said, "This is my beloved Son." The Church calls the light they saw the uncreated light, the very radiance of the divine nature. We are in the Dormition Fast, but fish is permitted today. All are welcome.

events.title: Spring Parish Cleanup Day
events.subtitle: Help us prepare the grounds for Pentecost
events.description: Join us for a morning of cleaning, gardening, and light maintenance around the cathedral grounds as we prepare for the Feast of Pentecost. Lunch provided for all volunteers. Bring work gloves if you have them.
